steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank is an independent clause (subject: Researcher Lin Zhi, verb: developed). The right side begins with a participial phrase (dissolving and reweaving...) followed by a second independent clause (subject: Zhi, verb: increased).

steps.title: Apply the rule for separating independent clauses
steps.content:
Two independent clauses require a period, semicolon, or comma-plus-coordinating-conjunction—never a comma alone.

A period after “silk” correctly applies this rule and allows the second sentence to open with “By dissolving...” as an introductory phrase.

Options A and B violate the rule by using insufficient punctuation, while option C creates an ungrammatical structure.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
